steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank connects two sentences that are in tension: Modernista architects championed nature, yet the building's specific features (wavy wall, botanical dressings) couldn't literally exist in a forest. The third sentence then uses "Still" to pivot back to genuine natural influence. The transition must acknowledge the first claim while conceding an exception.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Granted,: Signals a concession—it acknowledges the previous point (architects championed nature) while introducing a limitation (this particular building couldn't actually grow in a forest). This sets up "Still" in the next sentence perfectly. ✓
  • Choice B — Similarly,: Signals that the second point parallels the first. It would mean the building's features are another example of championing nature, but the sentence says the opposite—those features couldn't grow in a forest. ✗
  • Choice C — Furthermore,: Signals addition or reinforcement. It would treat the second sentence as extra evidence supporting the architects' love of nature, but the sentence actually introduces a contradiction. ✗
  • Choice D — Thus,: Signals a logical result or conclusion. There is no causal relationship between championing nature and the building's features not being able to grow in a forest. ✗
That is the correct answer.
